content.text: Tool selection is the model's decision about which tool (if any) to call given a user message and the available tool set. Getting selection right requires more than good tool descriptions — it also depends on how many tools are present, whether their scopes overlap, and what the system prompt says about when tools should or should not be used. Errors in tool selection fall into two types: selecting the wrong tool (confusion) and not selecting any tool when one is needed (omission), or the reverse.
Selection Failure Modes
Failure mode
Cause
Fix
Wrong tool selected
Overlapping descriptions; ambiguous tool names
Add negative scope to each description; use distinct names
No tool called (omission)
Trigger condition not clear in description
Add explicit "use this when" clause; use tool_choice: "any" if a tool is always needed
Tool called unnecessarily
Description too broad; model calls tool for questions it could answer directly
Add "use only when X" and "do not use for Y" to description
Multiple tools called in wrong order
Dependency between tools not communicated
State the dependency in the system prompt or tool description
Controlling Selection with tool_choice
Disambiguating Overlapping Tools
When two tools have related scopes, the model can confuse them. Add explicit negative-scope sentences to each description to carve out distinct decision boundaries.
System Prompt Guidance for Selection
For complex selection logic that cannot be expressed in individual tool descriptions, use the system prompt to state the selection policy explicitly.

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

SYSTEM = """You are a customer support agent. Tool selection rules:
1. Always try to answer from your own knowledge first.
2. Only call a tool if the answer requires live data (order status, account info, stock levels).
3. Never call search_orders and get_account_info in the same turn — resolve account questions first.
4. If unsure which tool to use, ask the user a clarifying question instead of guessing."""

response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
    max_tokens=200,
    system=SYSTEM,
    tools=TOOLS,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "What are your business hours?"}],
)
tool_calls = [b.name for b in response.content if b.type == "tool_use"]
text = next((b.text for b in response.content if hasattr(b, "text")), None)
print(f"Tool calls: {tool_calls}")
print(f"Response: {text}")

Tool selection quality is a function of three things in priority order: (1) description clarity — each tool must have an unambiguous trigger condition and scope; (2) system prompt policy — for multi-tool agents, state selection rules explicitly rather than relying on descriptions alone; (3) tool_choice override — use it when the application logic (not the model) should decide whether a tool runs. Testing selection with a representative set of user messages before deployment is the most reliable way to catch confusion and omission failures before they reach production.
